```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=60}
plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3 <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  sensi_1_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_3_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3$signif,
  sensi_1_3_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 4) 

rm(plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   bdd_danish_sensi_1_3, 
   all_vars_labels, 
   proteomic_sensi_1_3)

```

## Sensitivity 1 + 3 + 4: Removing NEFL outlier + filtering cases and their controls with follow-up \> 5 years + filtering follow-up \< 50%

### Logistic regressions

Conditional logistic regressions (continuous standardized), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-22

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_4 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-23
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Base model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_3_4 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered to follow-up>5years\n+Filtered to follow-up<50%\nBase model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)
  
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Adjusted model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_3_4 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered to follow-up>5years\n+Filtered to follow-up<50%\nAdjusted model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)
```

Conditional logistic regressions (quartiles), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-24
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_4 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

Generalized additive models modeling proteins as smooth terms (thin plate splines), base models adjusted on sex and age at baseline. Adjusted models further account for smoking and body mass index

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=60}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-25
#| column: screen-inset-right

proteomic_sensi_1_3_4 <-
  proteomic |> 
  str_replace("proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL", "proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_3_4")

bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_4 <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  group_by(match) |>                                                              
  filter(any(als == 1 & follow_up > 60)) |>                                     # sensi 3 : we remove cases (and their controls) with follow-up < 60 months
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(                                                                       # sensi 1 : we remove NfL in match 159 because it's an outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_3_4 = 
      ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>
  mutate(seuil = quantile(follow_up, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)) |>                     # sensi 4 : we remove 50% of the highest values of follow-up (after removing the follow-up<5years)
  group_by(match) |>
  filter(any(als == 1 & follow_up <= seuil)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-seuil) 


all_vars_labels <- set_names(
  str_replace(proteomic_sensi_1_3_4,
              "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
              ""),
  proteomic_sensi_1_3_4)

plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4 <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_3_4, make_gam_plot_base, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_4)
names(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4) <- proteomic_sensi_1_3_4
plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4 <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4 <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_3_4, make_gam_plot_adjusted, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_4)
names(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4) <- proteomic_sensi_1_3_4
plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4 <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  sensi_1_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_3_4_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4$signif,
  sensi_1_3_4_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 4) 

rm(plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   all_vars_labels, 
   proteomic_sensi_1_3_4,
   bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_4)

```

## Sensitivity 1 + 3 + 5: Removing NEFL outlier + filtering cases and their controls with follow-up \> 5 years + filtering follow-up \> 50%

### Logistic regressions

Conditional logistic regressions (continuous standardized), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-26

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_5 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-27
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Base model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_3_5 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered to follow-up>5years\n+Filtered to follow-up>50%\nBase model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)
  
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Adjusted model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_3_5 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered to follow-up>5years\n+Filtered to follow-up>50%\nAdjusted model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)
```

Conditional logistic regressions (quartiles), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-28
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_5 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

Generalized additive models modeling proteins as smooth terms (thin plate splines), base models adjusted on sex and age at baseline. Adjusted models further account for smoking and body mass index

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=60}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-29
#| column: screen-inset-right


proteomic_sensi_1_3_5 <-
  proteomic |> 
  str_replace("proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL", "proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_3_5")


bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_5 <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  group_by(match) |>                                                            # sensi 3 : we remove cases (and their controls) with follow-up < 60 months  
  filter(any(als == 1 & follow_up > 60)) |>
  ungroup()|>
  mutate(                                                                       # sensi 1 : we remove NfL in match 159 because it's an outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_3_5 = 
      ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>
  mutate(seuil = quantile(follow_up, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)) |>                     # sensi 5 : we remove 50% of the lowest values of follow-up (after removing the follow-up<5years)
  group_by(match) |>
  filter(any(als == 1 & follow_up > seuil)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-seuil)

all_vars_labels <- set_names(
  str_replace(proteomic_sensi_1_3_5,
              "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
              ""),
  proteomic_sensi_1_3_5)

plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5 <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_3_5, make_gam_plot_base, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_5)
names(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5) <- proteomic_sensi_1_3_5
plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5 <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5 <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_3_5, make_gam_plot_adjusted, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_5)
names(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5) <- proteomic_sensi_1_3_5
plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5 <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  sensi_1_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_3_5_base = plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5$signif,
  sensi_1_3_5_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 4) 

rm(plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   all_vars_labels, 
   proteomic_sensi_1_3_5,
   bdd_danish_sensi_1_3_5)

```

## Sensitivity 6: stratifiyng the analysis in tertiles of follow-up duration

Data not shown because of merging sensi 1 and sensi 6

```{r fig.height=12, fig.width=15}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-30
#| eval: false
#| include: false

### Logistic regressions
# Conditional logistic regressions (continuous standardized), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$main$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure + labs(title = "Main analysis - full sample size") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_6_T1 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T1 (<11 years)") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_6_T2 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T2 (11-16 years)") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_6_T3 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T3 (>16 years)") + theme(legend.position = "bottom") 
  
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$main$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure + labs(title = "Main analysis - full sample size") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_6_T1 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T1 (<11 years)") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_6_T2 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T2 (11-16 years)") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_6_T3 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T3 (>16 years)") + theme(legend.position = "bottom") 
```

## Sensitivity 1 + 6: Removing NEFL outlier + stratifiyng the analysis in tertiles of follow-up duration

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-31
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$sensi_6_table_follow_up
```

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=4}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-32
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_6$sensi_6_densityplot_follow_up
```

### Logistic regressions

Conditional logistic regressions (continuous standardized), matched on age at baseline and sex (further adjusted for smoking and body mass index)

```{r fig.width=15, fig.height=12}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-33
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1 + labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Base model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_6_T1 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T1 (<11 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Base model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_6_T2 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T2 (11-16 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Base model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_6_T3 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T3 (>16 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Base model") + theme(legend.position = "bottom")  
  
  
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1 + labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Adjusted model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_6_T1 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T1 (<11 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Adjusted model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_6_T2 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T2 (11-16 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Adjusted model") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_6$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_6_T3 + labs(title = "Filtered to follow-up T3 (>16 years)\n+ NEFL outlier excluded \n Adjusted model") + theme(legend.position = "bottom")  
```

NEFL is not significantly associated with ALS occurence when the follow-up duration is \>16 years. We don't see many associations in the follow-up group T1: I suspect that it is because of high heterogeneity in follow-up duration among this sub-group (0-11 years). --\> then I trust more the sensi 2 results where we just remove the cases (and their controls) with follow-up \<5 years.

## Sensitivity 1 + 7: Removing NEFL outlier + stratifiyng the analysis on sex

### Continuous coding (standardized)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-34
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_7$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_7
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-35
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Base model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_7$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_7  + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered on sex") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)
  
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier removed \n Adjusted model") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") + 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_7$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_7 + 
  labs(title = "NEFL outlier excluded\n+Filtered on sex") + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2)

```

### Quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-36
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$sensi_1_7$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_7
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.width=9, fig.height=70}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-37
#| column: screen-inset-right

proteomic_sensi_1_7_female <-
  proteomic |> 
  str_replace("proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL", "proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_7_female")

bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_female <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  group_by(match) |>                                                            # sensi 7 : we purrr::keep cases (and their controls) that are females 
  filter(any(als == 1 & sex == "Female")) |>
  ungroup()|>
  mutate(                                                                       # sensi 1 : we remove NfL in match 159 because it's an outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_7_female =
      ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL))



all_vars_labels <- set_names(
  str_replace(proteomic_sensi_1_7_female,
              "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
              ""),
  proteomic_sensi_1_7_female)


plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_7_female, make_gam_plot_base_sex, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_female)
names(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female) <- proteomic_sensi_1_7_female
plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_7_female, make_gam_plot_adjusted_sex, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_female)
names(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female) <- proteomic_sensi_1_7_female
plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female <- list(
  signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = purrr::keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


proteomic_sensi_1_7_male <-
  proteomic |> 
  str_replace("proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL", "proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_7_male")

bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_male <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  group_by(match) |>                                                            # sensi 7 : we keep cases (and their controls) that are male 
  filter(any(als == 1 & sex == "Male")) |>
  ungroup()|>
  mutate(                                                                       # sensi 1 : we remove NfL in match 159 because it's an outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL_sensi_1_7_male =
      ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL))


all_vars_labels <- set_names(
  str_replace(proteomic_sensi_1_7_male,
              "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
              ""),
  proteomic_sensi_1_7_male)

plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_7_male, make_gam_plot_base_sex, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_male)
names(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male) <- proteomic_sensi_1_7_male
plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male <- list(
  signif = keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = keep(plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male <- map(proteomic_sensi_1_7_male, make_gam_plot_adjusted_sex, data = bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_male)
names(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male) <- proteomic_sensi_1_7_male
plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male <- list(
  signif = keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$p_value <= 0.05) |> map("plot"),
  not_signif = keep(plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$p_value > 0.05) |> map("plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  sensi_1_adj  = plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1$signif,
  sensi_1_7_female = plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female$signif,
  sensi_1_7_male  = plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 3) 

rm(plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   all_vars_labels, 
   proteomic_sensi_1_7_female,
   proteomic_sensi_1_7_male,
   bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_female,
   bdd_danish_sensi_1_7_male)


```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-38
rm(   make_gam_plot_base, 
      make_gam_plot_adjusted,
      make_gam_plot_base_sex, 
      make_gam_plot_adjusted_sex, 
      plot_base_gam, 
      plot_adjusted_gam, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_2, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_2, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_3, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_3, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_4, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_4, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1_3_5, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_3_5, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_female, 
      plot_base_gam_sensi_1_7_male, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_female, 
      plot_adjusted_gam_sensi_1_7_male)
```

## Additionnal analysis 1

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=3}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-39
shapiro.test(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_1$OR_distribution$OR_log)

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_1$boxplot_OR
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_1$densityplot_OR
```

## Additionnal analysis 2

Testing the distribution of the p-values depending on OR\>=1 to the distribution of the p-values depending on OR\<1 using a Fasano-Franceschini test

The Fasano–Franceschini test is a multivariate extension of the Kolmogorov–Smirnov test. While the classical KS test only compares one-dimensional distributions, the Fasano–Franceschini test can compare two-dimensional or multidimensional distributions.

It works by evaluating, for every point of one dataset, how the cumulative distribution differs from the other dataset in the four quadrants around that point. The test statistic D represents the largest difference between the empirical multivariate distributions of the two datasets. A large value of D indicates that the two datasets occupy different regions of the multidimensional space (e.g., different shapes, locations, or dispersions).

The null hypothesis (H₀) is: H₀: The two datasets come from the same multivariate distribution. A small p-value indicates a significant deviation between the two distributions.

### all proteins

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-40
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_all_figure
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_all_results

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$jackknife_all_results |> 
  select(d_stat_percent) |> 
  mutate(d_stat_percent = round(d_stat_percent, 1)) |>
  tbl_summary()


```

Because the p-value is 0.00587, which is well below the standard 0.05 threshold, we reject the null hypothesis that the two sets of results (OR ≥ 1 vs. OR \< 1) come from the same distribution.

This means that the two groups of proteins, those associated with increased risk and those associated with decreased risk, show significantly different joint distributions of odds ratios and p-values. Therefore, the asymmetric patterns we observe are unlikely to be due to random fluctuation caused by multiple testing. The results support the idea that the proteins associated with increased disease risk and those associated with decreased disease risk represent genuinely different biological signals, rather than artifacts of high-dimensional multiple testing.

### Immune response

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-41
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_immune_figure
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_immune_results

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$jackknife_immune_results |> 
  select(d_stat_percent) |> 
  mutate(d_stat_percent = round(d_stat_percent, 1)) |>
  tbl_summary()
```

### Metbolism

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-42
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_metabolism_figure
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_metabolism_results

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$jackknife_metabolism_results |> 
  select(d_stat_percent) |> 
  mutate(d_stat_percent = round(d_stat_percent, 1)) |>
  tbl_summary()

```

### Neuro explo

```{r, fig.height=3, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-43
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_neuro_figure
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$additional_analysis_2_neuro_results

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence$additional_analysis_2$jackknife_neuro_results |> 
  select(d_stat_percent) |> 
  mutate(d_stat_percent = round(d_stat_percent, 1)) |>
  tbl_summary()
```

## Additional analysis 3 - superlearner

### Test 1 a : method AUC, many algorithms, no pre screening, default hyperparameters

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-44
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_a)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_a) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1a)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

Leeklasso is doing the best job --\> means that the relations are probably linear and for only a few proteins (because this algorithm has a internal prescreening)

Trees methodologies are not doing a great job but there are supposed to handle correctly high dimension: maybe not a lot of interactions between the proteins? But should investigate with tunned parameters

Algorithms such as SL.bayesglm, SL.stepAIC, SL.ksvm, SL.gam, SL.polymars and SL.earth should do better wit pre screening

### Defining the best hyperparameter combinaison for each algorithm

#### SL.glmnet

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-45
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_glmnet)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_glmnet) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.glmnet)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.ranger

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-46
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_ranger)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_ranger) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.ranger)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.xgboost

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-47
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_xgboost)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>

  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_xgboost) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.xgboost)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.ksvm

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-48
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_ksvm)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.ksvm)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.earth

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-49
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_earth)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_earth) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.earth)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

### Test 1 b : method AUC, many algorithms, pre screening, optimized hyperparameters

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-50
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_b_opt_20)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_b_opt_20) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1b top 20)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-51
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_b_opt_10)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_b_opt_10) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1b top 10)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

### Test 1 c : method AUC, selected algorithms, pre screening, optimized hyperparameters

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-52
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1c top 20)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-53
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_10)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_10) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1c top 10)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

### Weights of the algorithms (sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-54
# Calcul des poids moyens en pourcentage
all_weights <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20$AllSL, function(x) x$coef)
weights_matrix <- do.call(rbind, all_weights)
colnames(weights_matrix) <- results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20$libraryNames

df_weights <- data.frame(
  Algorithms = colnames(weights_matrix),
  Mean_weights = colMeans(weights_matrix) * 100) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Mean_weights))

df_weights |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(Mean_weights = "Mean weights (%)") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_weights", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(all_weights, weights_matrix, df_weights)
```

### Selected proteins by leeklasso (sl_fit_CV_1_c_All)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-55
# Extraction des protéines et coefficients pour Leekasso
leek_results_list <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20$AllSL, function(fold_fit) {
  obj <- fold_fit$fitLibrary$SL.leekasso_All$object
  if(is.null(obj)) return(NULL)
  
  coefs <- coef(obj)
  coefs <- coefs[names(coefs) != "(Intercept)"]
  
  data.frame(
    Protein = names(coefs),
    Log_Odds = as.numeric(coefs)
  )
})

df_leek_final <- do.call(rbind, leek_results_list) |>
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    Consistency = n() / 10 * 100,
    Mean_OR = exp(mean(Log_Odds)) ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Consistency))


df_leek_final |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Protein = "Selected proteins",
    Consistency = "Consistency across folds (%)", 
    Mean_OR = "Mean ORs across folds") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_OR", digits = 2) |>
  colformat_double(j = "Consistency", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(leek_results_list, df_leek_final)
```

### Selected proteins by GLMNet (sl_fit_CV_1_c_All)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-56
# Extraction des protéines selectionnées par GLMNet dans chaque pli
glm_results_list <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_1$sl_fit_CV_1_c_opt_20$AllSL, function(fold_fit) {
  fit_glmnet <- fold_fit$fitLibrary$SL.glmnet.opt_All$object
  coefs <- as.matrix(coef(fit_glmnet, s = "lambda.min"))
  
  data.frame(
    Protein = rownames(coefs),
    Log_Odds = as.numeric(coefs)
  ) |>
    dplyr::filter(Protein != "(Intercept)")
})

df_glm_final <- do.call(rbind, glm_results_list) |>
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    Consistency = sum(Log_Odds != 0) / 10 * 100,
    Mean_OR = exp(mean(Log_Odds[Log_Odds != 0]))) |>
  dplyr::filter(Consistency > 0) |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Consistency))

df_glm_final |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Protein = "Proteine",
    Consistency = "Consistency across folds (%)", 
    Mean_OR = "Mean ORs across folds") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_OR", digits = 2) |>
  colformat_double(j = "Consistency", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(glm_results_list, df_glm_final)
```

APEX1: DNA repair enzyme (Apurinic/Apyrimidinic Endonuclease 1)

NPTXR: Neuronal Pentraxin Receptor (involved in synaptic remodeling and glutamatergic signaling)

NfL: Marker of neuroaxonal damage

ARG1 (Arginase-1): Marker of alternative (M2) macrophage/microglial polarization

CD33: Myeloid cell surface antigen (Microglial marker)

PIK3AP1 (Phosphoinositide 3-kinase adapter protein 1): link between B-cell receptors and the PI3K signaling pathway

PSIP1 (PC4 and SFRS1-interacting protein 1): transcriptional coactivator that promotes cell survival (protecting cells against oxidative stress)

GLRX (Glutaredoxin-1): small redox enzyme that uses glutathione to reduce oxidized proteins (disulfides) (maintaining redox homeostasis)

IL-32 (Interleukin-32): Pro-inflammatory cytokine

NPM1 (Nucleophosmin): multifunctional "chaperone" protein found mainly in the nucleolus. it is involved in ribosome biogenesis, DNA repair, and protein folding.

GHRL (Ghrelin): metabolic peptide produced mainly in the stomach that regulates appetite and energy balance.

CD302: C-type lectin receptor involved in cell adhesion and migration, primarily expressed on myeloid cells like macrophages and dendritic cells.

PADI2: Protein-arginine deiminase type-2

### Test 2: + time as a predictor

### Defining the best hyperparameter combinaison for each algorithm

#### SL.glmnet

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-57
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_glmnet)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_glmnet) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.glmnet)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.ranger

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-58
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_ranger)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_ranger) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.ranger)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.xgboost

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-59
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_xgboost)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_xgboost) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.xgboost)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.ksvm

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-60
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_ksvm)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_ksvm) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.ksvm)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### SL.earth

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-61
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_earth)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_earth) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Specific to SL.earth)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Hyperparameter combinaisons", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

### Test 2 b : method AUC, many algorithms, pre screening, optimized hyperparameters

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-62
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_b_opt_20)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-63

  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>

  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>

       subtitle = "ALS detection",
```
