### Test 2 c : method AUC, selected algorithms, pre screening, optimized hyperparameters

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-64
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_20)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_20) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1c top 20)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-65
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1c top 10)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```


### Weights of the algorithms ()

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-66
# Calcul des poids moyens en pourcentage
all_weights <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10$AllSL, function(x) x$coef)
weights_matrix <- do.call(rbind, all_weights)
colnames(weights_matrix) <- results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10$libraryNames

df_weights <- data.frame(
  Algorithms = colnames(weights_matrix),
  Mean_weights = colMeans(weights_matrix) * 100) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Mean_weights))

df_weights |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(Mean_weights = "Mean weights (%)") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_weights", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(all_weights, weights_matrix, df_weights)
```

### Selected proteins by leeklasso

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-67
# Extraction des protéines et coefficients pour Leekasso
leek_results_list <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10$AllSL, function(fold_fit) {
  obj <- fold_fit$fitLibrary$SL.leekasso_All$object
  if(is.null(obj)) return(NULL)
  
  coefs <- coef(obj)
  coefs <- coefs[names(coefs) != "(Intercept)"]
  
  data.frame(
    Protein = names(coefs),
    Log_Odds = as.numeric(coefs)
  )
})

df_leek_final <- do.call(rbind, leek_results_list) |>
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    Consistency = n() / 10 * 100,
    Mean_OR = exp(mean(Log_Odds)) ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Consistency))


df_leek_final |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Protein = "Selected proteins",
    Consistency = "Consistency across folds (%)", 
    Mean_OR = "Mean ORs across folds") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_OR", digits = 2) |>
  colformat_double(j = "Consistency", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(leek_results_list, df_leek_final)
```

### Selected proteins by GLMNet

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-occurence-68
# Extraction des protéines selectionnées par GLMNet dans chaque pli
glm_results_list <- lapply(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_SL$test_2$sl_fit_CV_2_c_opt_10$AllSL, function(fold_fit) {
  fit_glmnet <- fold_fit$fitLibrary$SL.glmnet.a0.9_Al$object
  coefs <- as.matrix(coef(fit_glmnet, s = "lambda.min"))
  
  data.frame(
    Protein = rownames(coefs),
    Log_Odds = as.numeric(coefs)
  ) |>
    dplyr::filter(Protein != "(Intercept)")
})

df_glm_final <- do.call(rbind, glm_results_list) |>
  dplyr::group_by(Protein) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    Consistency = sum(Log_Odds != 0) / 10 * 100,
    Mean_OR = exp(mean(Log_Odds[Log_Odds != 0]))) |>
  dplyr::filter(Consistency > 0) |>
  dplyr::arrange(desc(Consistency))

df_glm_final |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Protein = "Proteine",
    Consistency = "Consistency across folds (%)", 
    Mean_OR = "Mean ORs across folds") |>
  colformat_double(j = "Mean_OR", digits = 2) |>
  colformat_double(j = "Consistency", digits = 1) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |>
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  autofit()

rm(glm_results_list, df_glm_final)
```

## Additional analysis 4 - tidymodels 

### Test 1: Can plasma proteins predict ALS? (proteins + covariates: birth year, sex, BMI, smoking)

For each test, the pipeline follows two phases:

-   Hyperparameter tuning: each algorithm is independently optimized via cross-validation.

-   Model comparison: the best-tuned version of each algorithm is evaluated on the same folds and ranked by AUC.

All models use a grouped 10-cross-validation respecting the matching structure: all 3 individuals from a same triplet (1 case + 2 controls) are always kept on the same side — either all in the training set or all in the test set. Without this grouping, the model could learn patterns tied to the matching variables (birth year and sex) rather than the true proteomic signal, artificially inflating AUC.

#### Penalized Logistic Regression (glmnet) tuning
Standard logistic regression would fail here because p \> n (\~280 predictors, 166 cases). glmnet solves this by adding a penalty term to the loss function that shrinks coefficients toward zero, effectively performing implicit variable selection.

Two hyperparameters are tuned:

-   penalty (lambda): Strength of regularization. Higher lambda = more coefficients forced to zero.

-   mixture (alpha): Type of penalty.

    -   alpha = 1 = Lasso (exact zeros, variable selection).

    -   alpha = 0 = Ridge (all coefficients shrunk but non-zero).

    -   Between 0 and 1 = Elastic Net.

Tuning method: tune_bayes(). Instead of exhaustively testing all combinations on a fixed grid, Bayesian optimization builds a probabilistic model of the performance surface and intelligently focuses evaluations on promising regions. 40 iterations explore the penalty x mixture space far more efficiently than a 7 x 20 = 140-point regular grid.

```{r}
#| label: test1-glmnet
autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_glmnet, type = "performance") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  ggtitle("Test 1 – glmnet (tune_bayes)")

show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_glmnet, 
          metric = "roc_auc",
          n = 5)|>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best glmnet T1 — mixture (alpha):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_glmnet$mixture, 3),
    "| penalty (lambda):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_glmnet$penalty, 5), "\n")
```

#### Random forest tuning

Random Forest trains 1,000 decision trees independently, each on a random bootstrap sample of the data and using only a random subset of predictors at each split. The final prediction is the average across all 1,000 trees.This double randomization makes Random Forest highly resistant to overfitting.

Three hyperparameters are tuned:

-   mtry: Number of predictors randomly considered at each split. Explored between 10% and 30% of total predictors.

-   min_n: Minimum number of observations in a terminal node. It controls tree depth and complexity.

-   splitrule: Criterion used to choose the best split. "gini" = standard Gini impurity. "extratrees" = thresholds are drawn randomly, adding more randomization, often better in high dimensions. Both are tested and the best is kept.

Tuning method: tune_bayes() for mtry and min_n (continuous parameters). splitrule is discrete so the code loops over both values and selects the best.

```{r}
#| label: test1-rf
cat("Best RF T1 — splitrule:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_rf_t1$splitrule,
    "| mtry:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_rf_t1$best$mtry,
    "| min_n:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_rf_t1$best$min_n, "\n")
```

#### Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) tuning

XGBoost builds trees sequentially: each tree corrects the residual errors of the previous one. This sequential method generally yields higher accuracy than random forest but makes XGBoost more prone to overfitting and sensitive to more hyperparameters.

Tuning was done in two steps:

-   Step 1 — Structural hyperparameters:

    -   tree_depth: Maximum depth of each tree (1–4). Kept shallow to prevent overfitting with small data.

    -   learn_rate (eta): Step size for each correction (0.001–0.1). Smaller = more conservative learning.

    -   mtry (colsample_bytree): Fraction of predictors used per tree.

    -   min_n (min_child_weight): Minimum complexity required to create a new node.

    -   loss_reduction (gamma): Minimum gain required to make an additional split — acts as an extra regularizer.

    -   sample_size (subsample): Fixed at 0.8: 80% of rows sampled per tree. Not tuned (standard value).

-   Step 2 — Number of trees via early stopping (xgb.cv, not available with tidymodels):

    -   Once the best structural hyperparameters are fixed, xgb.cv() runs up to 5,000 boosting rounds but stops automatically (early_stopping_rounds = 50) if the CV AUC has not improved for 50 consecutive rounds. This prevents both underfitting (too few trees) and overfitting (too many trees).

    -   The same fold indices as tidymodels are passed to xgb.cv() to ensure the CV is strictly comparable.

```{r}
#| label: test1-xgboost
autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_xgb_t1) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  ggtitle("Test 1 – XGBoost")

show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_xgb_t1, 
          metric = "roc_auc", 
          n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  flextable::fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best XGB structural params T1:\n"); results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_xgb_params_t1 |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  flextable::fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

# 
# cat("AUC CV (mean ± SD):", 
#     round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean[results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_xgb_trees_t1], 3),
#     "±", 
#     round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_std[results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_xgb_trees_t1], 3), "\n")
```

```{r fig.height=5}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# Courbe d'apprentissage
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log |>
  ggplot(aes(x = iter)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$train_auc_mean, color = "Train")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean,  color = "CV Test")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean - results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_std,
                  ymax = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean + results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$xgb_cv_es_t1$evaluation_log$test_auc_std), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_xgb_trees_t1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = "Learning curve XGBoost – Test 1",
       x = "Nb of trees", y = "AUC", color = "") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### Linear Support Vector Machine (SMV) tuning

The SVM finds the hyperplane that best separates cases from controls by maximizing the margin between the two groups.

"Linear" means the decision boundary is a linear combination of predictors.

One hyperparameter is tuned:

-   cost: Trade-off between maximizing the margin and penalizing misclassified points.

    -   Low cost = wide margin, more errors tolerated (strong regularization).

    -   High cost = narrow margin, fewer errors tolerated.

Applied on the top-20 filtered recipe because SVM is numerically unstable in p \> n settings.

```{r}
#| label: test1-smv
show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_svm_t1, metric = "roc_auc", n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best SVM T1 — cost:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_svm_t1$cost, 4), "\n")
```

#### Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) tuning

MARS is a non-linear model that automatically discovers breakpoints in predictor-outcome relationships. For example, it can learn "if protein X \> threshold, the effect is strong; otherwise it is negligible". More flexible than linear models but more interpretable than tree ensembles.

Two hyperparameters are tuned:

-   num_terms: Number of terms in the model — controls complexity (5, 10, 20, 50 tested).

-   prod_degree: Degree of interactions: 1 = additive model only, 2 = pairwise interactions allowed.

Applied on the top-20 filtered recipe because MARS is numerically unstable in p \> n settings.

```{r}
#| label: test1-mars
show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$tune_mars_t1, metric = "roc_auc", n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
```

#### Comparaison of the best combinaisons Test 1

Once the best hyperparameters are identified for each algorithm, all models are evaluated on the same 10 cross-validation folds. This ensures a fair comparison: same data splits, same metric (AUC).

```{r}
#| label: test1-comparison-best-combinaisons
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$summary_t1 |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$results_t1, metric = "roc_auc") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean, 3)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Test 1 – Comparaison of the models (AUC, grouped 10-fold)",
       x = "Mean AUC (CV)", y = "")
```

#### Best model Test 1

```{r}
#| label: test1-best-model
cat("Best model Test 1:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$best_wf_id_t1, "\n")
cat("   Mean AUC:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$summary_t1$mean[1], 3),
    "± SE:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$summary_t1$std_err[1], 3), "\n")
```

#### Interpretation : glmnet (winner) + XGBoost (comparison)

##### Glmnet

Top 89 predictors (by \|coefficient\|) selected by glmnet elasticnet

```{r, fig.height=15}
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$t_glmnet_t1
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$f_glmnet_t1
```

##### XGboost

Top 20 variables by SHAP importance

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$t_xgboost_t1
```

SHAP beeswarm plot

```{r, fig.height=12, fig.width=12}
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$f_xgb_beeswarm_t1
```

SHAP bar plot

```{r}
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$f_xgb_barplot_t1
```

Dependence plots (top 6)

```{r, fig.height=25, fig.width=8}
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$f_xgb_dependanceplot_t1 |> 
  wrap_plots(ncol = 1, nrow = 6)
```

#### Comparison: glmnet vs XGBoost across both tests

```{r}
cat("COMPARISON: glmnet vs XGBoost COEFFICIENTS/SHAP\n")
cat("  Common top 20 proteins:", length(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$overlap_t1), "\n")
if (length(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$overlap_t1) > 0) {
  cat("  Proteins:", paste(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_1$overlap_t1, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}
```


### Test 2: Can plasma proteins predict ALS? (proteins + follow-up + covariates: birth year, sex, BMI, smoking) 
#### Penalized Logistic Regression (glmnet) tuning

Standard logistic regression would fail here because p \> n (\~280 predictors, 166 cases). glmnet solves this by adding a penalty term to the loss function that shrinks coefficients toward zero, effectively performing implicit variable selection.

Two hyperparameters are tuned:

-   penalty (lambda): Strength of regularization. Higher lambda = more coefficients forced to zero.

-   mixture (alpha): Type of penalty.

    -   alpha = 1 = Lasso (exact zeros, variable selection).

    -   alpha = 0 = Ridge (all coefficients shrunk but non-zero).

    -   Between 0 and 1 = Elastic Net.

Tuning method: tune_bayes(). Instead of exhaustively testing all combinations on a fixed grid, Bayesian optimization builds a probabilistic model of the performance surface and intelligently focuses evaluations on promising regions. 40 iterations explore the penalty x mixture space far more efficiently than a 7 x 20 = 140-point regular grid.

```{r}
#| label: test2-glmnet
autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_glmnet, type = "performance") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  ggtitle("Test 2 – glmnet (tune_bayes)")

show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_glmnet, 
          metric = "roc_auc",
          n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best glmnet T2 — mixture (alpha):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_glmnet$mixture, 3),
    "| penalty (lambda):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_glmnet$penalty, 5), "\n")
```

#### Random forest tuning

```{r}
#| label: test2-rf
cat("Best RF T2 — splitrule:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_rf_t2$splitrule,
    "| mtry:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_rf_t2$best$mtry,
    "| min_n:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_rf_t2$best$min_n, "\n")
```

#### Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) tuning

XGBoost builds trees sequentially: each tree corrects the residual errors of the previous one. This sequential method generally yields higher accuracy than random forest but makes XGBoost more prone to overfitting and sensitive to more hyperparameters.

Tuning was done in two steps:

-   Step 1 — Structural hyperparameters:

    -   tree_depth: Maximum depth of each tree (1–4). Kept shallow to prevent overfitting with small data.

    -   learn_rate (eta): Step size for each correction (0.001–0.1). Smaller = more conservative learning.

    -   mtry (colsample_bytree): Fraction of predictors used per tree.

    -   min_n (min_child_weight): Minimum complexity required to create a new node.

    -   loss_reduction (gamma): Minimum gain required to make an additional split — acts as an extra regularizer.

    -   sample_size (subsample): Fixed at 0.8: 80% of rows sampled per tree. Not tuned (standard value).

-   Step 2 — Number of trees via early stopping (xgb.cv, not available with tidymodels):

    -   Once the best structural hyperparameters are fixed, xgb.cv() runs up to 5,000 boosting rounds but stops automatically (early_stopping_rounds = 50) if the CV AUC has not improved for 50 consecutive rounds. This prevents both underfitting (too few trees) and overfitting (too many trees).

    -   The same fold indices as tidymodels are passed to xgb.cv() to ensure the CV is strictly comparable.

```{r}
#| label: test2-xgboost
autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_xgb_t2) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  ggtitle("Test 2 – XGBoost")

show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_xgb_t2, 
          metric = "roc_auc", 
          n = 5)|>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  flextable::fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best XGB structural params T2:\n"); results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_xgb_params_t2 |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  flextable::fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

# 
# cat("CV AUC (mean ± SD):", 
#     round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean[results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_xgb_trees_t2], 3),
#     "±", 
#     round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_std[results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_xgb_trees_t2], 3), "\n")
```

```{r fig.height=5}
#| eval: false
#| include: false
# Courbe d'apprentissage
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log |>
  ggplot(aes(x = iter)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$train_auc_mean, color = "Train")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean,  color = "CV Test")) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean - results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_std,
                  ymax = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean + results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$xgb_cv_es_t2$evaluation_log$test_auc_std), 
              alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_xgb_trees_t2, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  labs(title = "Learning curve XGBoost – Test 2",
       x = "Nb of trees", y = "AUC", color = "") +
  theme_minimal()
```

#### Linear Support Vector Machine (SMV) tuning

The SVM finds the hyperplane that best separates cases from controls by maximizing the margin between the two groups.

"Linear" means the decision boundary is a linear combination of predictors.

One hyperparameter is tuned:

-   cost: Trade-off between maximizing the margin and penalizing misclassified points.

    -   Low cost = wide margin, more errors tolerated (strong regularization).

    -   High cost = narrow margin, fewer errors tolerated.

Applied on the top-20 filtered recipe because SVM is numerically unstable in p \> n settings.

```{r}
#| label: test2-smv
show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_svm_t2, metric = "roc_auc", n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best SVM T2 — cost:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_svm_t2$cost, 4), "\n")
```

#### Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) tuning

MARS is a non-linear model that automatically discovers breakpoints in predictor-outcome relationships. For example, it can learn "if protein X \> threshold, the effect is strong; otherwise it is negligible". More flexible than linear models but more interpretable than tree ensembles.

Two hyperparameters are tuned:

-   num_terms: Number of terms in the model — controls complexity (5, 10, 20, 50 tested).

-   prod_degree: Degree of interactions: 1 = additive model only, 2 = pairwise interactions allowed.

Applied on the top-20 filtered recipe because MARS is numerically unstable in p \> n settings.

```{r}
#| label: test2-mars
show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$tune_mars_t2, metric = "roc_auc", n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
```

#### Comparaison of the best combinaisons Test 2

Once the best hyperparameters are identified for each algorithm, all models are evaluated on the same 10 cross-validation folds. This ensures a fair comparison: same data splits, same metric (AUC).

```{r}
#| label: test2-comparison-best-combinaisons
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$summary_t2 |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$results_t2, metric = "roc_auc") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean, 3)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Test 2 – Model comparaison (AUC, grouped 10-fold)", 
       x = "Mean AUC", y = "")
```

#### Best model Test 2

```{r}
#| label: test2-best-model
cat("Best model Test 2 :", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$best_wf_id_t2, "\n")
cat("   Mean AUC:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$summary_t2$mean[1], 3),
    "± SE:", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$summary_t2$std_err[1], 3), "\n")
```

#### Interpreation : glmnet (winner) + XGBoost (comparison)

##### Glmnet

Top 110 predictors (by \|coefficient\|)

```{r, fig.height=18}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-1
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$t_glmnet_t2
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$p_glmnet_t2
```

##### XGboost

Top 20 variables by SHAP importance

```{r, fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-2
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$t_xgboost_t2
```

SHAP beeswarm plot

```{r, fig.height=12, fig.width=12}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-3
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$f_xgb_beeswarm_t2
```

SHAP bar plot

```{r}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-4
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$f_xgb_barplot_t2
```

Dependence plots (top 6)

```{r, fig.height=25, fig.width=8}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-5
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$f_xgb_dependanceplot_t2 |> 
  wrap_plots(ncol = 1, nrow = 6)
```

Dependence plots by follow-up duration (top 6)

```{r, fig.height=25, fig.width=8}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-6
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$f_xgb_dependanceplot_by_followup_t2 |> 
  wrap_plots(ncol = 1, nrow = 6)
```

Interaction follow_up × protéines

```{r, fig.height=9, fig.width=8}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-7
#| column: screen-inset-right
cat("\nSHAP |value| by follow-up tertile:\n")
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$f_fu_shap
```

Trajectory analysis

```{r}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-8
cat("\nProtein signal trajectory:\n")
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$shap_fu_trend 
```

#### Comparison: glmnet vs XGBoost across both tests

```{r}
#| label: Interpreation:glmnet(winner)+XGBoost(comparison)-9
cat("COMPARISON: glmnet vs XGBoost COEFFICIENTS/SHAP\n")
cat("  Common top 20 proteins:", length(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$overlap_t2), "\n")
if (length(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$overlap_t2) > 0) {
  cat("  Proteins:", paste(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_2$overlap_t2, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}
```


### Test 3: interaction with follow-up duration?
#### Comparison of glmnet models 
```{r}
#| label: tidymodel-test-3-1
autoplot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$tune_glmnet, type = "performance") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  ggtitle("Test 3 – glmnet (tune_bayes)")

show_best(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$tune_glmnet, 
          metric = "roc_auc",
          n = 5) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

cat("Best glmnet T3 — mixture (alpha):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$best_glmnet$mixture, 3),
    "| penalty (lambda):", 
    round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$best_glmnet$penalty, 5), "\n")
```

#### Interpretation of the best glmnet model
```{r}
#| label: tidymodel-test-3-2
# Extraction de la valeur numérique moyenne du meilleur AUC
best_auc_value <- show_best(
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$tune_glmnet_t3, 
  metric = "roc_auc", 
  n = 1)$mean

# Récupération des prédictions hors-échantillon
best_predictions_t3 <- 
  results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$tune_glmnet_t3 |> 
  collect_predictions(
    parameters = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$best_glmnet_t3)

# Calcul des points de la courbe ROC
roc_curve_data_t3 <- 
  best_predictions_t3 |>
  roc_curve(truth = als, .pred_case)

# plot
roc_curve_data_t3 |> 
  autoplot() +
  labs(
    title = "AUC curve - Test 3 (Elastic Net with follow-up interactions)",
    subtitle = paste0("CV 10-folds — Mean AUC: ", round(best_auc_value, 3))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40", size = 10))


rm(best_auc_value, best_predictions_t3, roc_curve_data_t3)
```

#### Extraction of the terms selected by elastic net 
```{r}
#| label: tidymodel-test-3-3
# if(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$glmnet_t3_result$success) {
#   cat("\n--- Test 3 results ---")
#   cat("\nBest hyperparameters:")
#   cat("\n  - Alpha (mixture):", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$best_glmnet_t3$mixture, 3))
#   cat("\n  - Lambda (penalty):", round(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$best_glmnet_t3$penalty, 5))
#   cat("\nNumber of selected terms by elastic net:", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$glmnet_t3_result$n_selected, "\n")
#   
#   print(head(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$glmnet_t3_result$coefs_all, 143))
# } else {
#   cat("Erreur lors de l'extraction des coefficients :", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$glmnet_t3_result$error, "\n")
# }


# Top 143 predictors (by |coefficient|)
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_tidymodels$test_3$glmnet_t3_result$coefs_protein |> 
  slice_head(n = 143) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(Feature = str_remove(Feature, "proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_metabolism_"), 
         Feature = str_replace(Feature, "_x_follow_up_no_na_y", " x follow-up")) |>
  select(-abs_coef, -direction, - coefficient_raw) |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  flextable::fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()

```



# Proteomic and years to ALS

## Cox models among cases

Event = ALS, time to event = from baseline to ALS diagnostic (cases)


### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=5}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-1
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_cox$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_cox |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| column: screen-inset-right
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-2
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_cox$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_cox, 
           results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_cox$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_cox, 
           ncol = 2)
```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-3
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_cox$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_cox |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-4
bdd_danish_cases <- bdd_danish |> filter(als == 1)

surv_obj <- Surv(time = bdd_danish_cases$follow_up, event = bdd_danish_cases$als)

vars_labels <- 
  set_names(str_replace(proteomic, 
                        "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_", 
                        ""), 
            proteomic)


#### Base ----
cox_gam_results_base <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_danish_cases, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("sex", "baseline_age"), 
                       title = "Base model")

#### Adjusted ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_danish_cases, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("sex", "baseline_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish <- list(
  signif = map(keep(cox_gam_results_base, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(keep(cox_gam_results_base, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))

plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish <- list(
  signif = map(keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))

rm(vars_labels, surv_obj, bdd_danish_cases, cox_gam_results_base, cox_gam_results_adjusted)
```

```{r fig.height=20, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-5
plots_lists <- list(
  base  = plot_base_cox_gam_danish$signif, 
  adj  = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, plot_base_cox_gam_danish, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish)

```

## Fixed-horizon logistic analyses

### Proteome wide associations (logistic regressions)

#### Continuous coding (standardized)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-6
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_y_10_20
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-7
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_y_10ans
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-8
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_y_20ans
```

#### Quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-9
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$proteome_wide$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_y_10_20
```

### Machine learning (Xboost)

#### 10 years horizon

Number of optimal trees

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-10
cat("---> Optimal number of trees (10 years horizon) :", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$best_nrounds_10, "\n")
```

Maximum AUC (Cross-validated)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-11
cat("---> Maximum AUC (cross validated, 10 years horizon) :", max(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_10ans$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean), "\n")
```

Importance top 20 variables (10 years horizon)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-12
cat("\nTop 20 predictors - 10 years horizon :\n")
head(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$importance_10, 20) |>
    flextable() |>
  merge_h(part = "header") |>
  theme_vanilla() |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-13
xgb.plot.importance(
  importance_matrix = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$importance_10[1:20, ], 
  main = "Importance of the top 20 predictors (10 years horizon)")
```

#### 20 years horizon

Number of optimal trees

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-14
cat("---> Optimal number of trees (20 years horizon) :", results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$best_nrounds_20, "\n")
```

Maximum AUC (Cross-validated)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-15
cat("---> Maximum AUC (cross validated, 20 years horizon) :", max(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_20ans$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean), "\n")
```

Importance top 20 variables (20 year horizon)

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-16
cat("\nTop 20 predictors - 20 years horizon :\n")
head(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$importance_20, 20) |>
    flextable() |>
  merge_h(part = "header") |>
  theme_vanilla() |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all")|> 
  set_table_properties(align = "left")
```

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-17
xgb.plot.importance(
  importance_matrix = results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$fixed_horizon_logistic_regression_analysis$machine_learning_xgboost$importance_20[1:20, ], 
  main = "Importance of the top 20 predictors (20 years horizon)")
```

## Logistic models including time

### Proteome wide associations

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-18
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$proteome_wide$proteomic_interaction_table
```

### Machine learning (XGboost)

#### Etape 1 : choisir les meilleurs hyperparameters avec super learner (excepté nb de trees) 
```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-19
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_AUC)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_AUC) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1 to choose the best hyperparameters except nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")

summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_NNLS)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_NNLS)  + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1 to choose the best hyperparameters except nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "V-fold CV Risk Estimate (NNLS)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.222, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

$$MSE_{hasard} = p(1-p) = \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{3} = \frac{2}{9} \approx 0.222$$


#### Etape 2 : choisir le nb de trees avec SL (grace a l'argument early_stopping_rounds ) et les meilleurs hyperparameters choisis en 1
```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-20
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_2_AUC)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_2_AUC) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 2 to choose the best nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")

summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_2_NNLS)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_2_NNLS) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 2 to choose the best nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "V-fold CV Risk Estimate (NNLS)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.222, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

#### Etapes 1 et 2 mixées ensembles
```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-21
summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_2_AUC)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_2_AUC) + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1 and 2 mixed to choose the best hyperparameters and nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "Cross-Validated AUC") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")

summary(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_2_NNLS)$Table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  flextable() |> 
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left") |>
  autofit()
plot(results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$logistic_models_including_time$machine_learning_xgboost$cv_sl_1_2_NNLS)  + 
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Model Performance (Step 1 and 2 mixed to choose the best hyperparameters and nb of trees)", 
       subtitle = "ALS detection",
       x = "Algorithm", y = "V-fold CV Risk Estimate (NNLS)") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.222, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

## Linear models 
### Proteome wide associations 
```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-22
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_linear$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_linear 
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_linear$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_linear
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_linear$proteome_wide$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_linear 
```

### Machine learning (tidymodels)
```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-y-to-ALS-23
results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_linear$machine_learning$t_glmnet_reg

results_proteomic_ALS_occurrence_y_to_als$main_results_linear$machine_learning$f_glmnet_reg 
```

# Proteomic and ALS survival

## Effects of subject characteristics on ALS survival

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-1
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$main$covar |>
  as_flex_table() |>
  flextable::font(fontname = "Calibri", part = "all") |> 
  fontsize(size = 10, part = "all") |>
  padding(padding.top = 0, padding.bottom = 0, part = "all") |>
  set_table_properties(align = "left")
```

## Main analyses

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=5}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-2
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$main$proteomic_sd_ALS_table |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-3
#| column: screen-inset-right

wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$main$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure, 
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$main$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure, 
           ncol = 2)
```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-4
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$main$proteomic_quart_ALS_table  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=55, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-5
fit_and_plot_cox_gam <- function(
    data,
    vars = proteomic,
    time = "follow_up_death",
    status = "status_death", 
    covariates = c("sex", "diagnosis_age"),
    vars_labels = NULL,
    y_limits = c(-12, 12),
    title = "Base model"
) {
  
  if (is.null(vars_labels)) {
    vars_labels <- set_names(vars, vars)
  }
  
  fit_one <- function(var) {
    
    outcome <- cbind(data[[time]], data[[status]])
    formula_str <- paste0(
      "outcome ~ s(", var, ") + ",
      paste(covariates, collapse = " + "))
    
    model <- mgcv::gam(as.formula(formula_str),
                       data = data,
                       family = cox.ph())
  
    smry <- summary(model)
    edf_val <- smry$s.table[1, "edf"]
    pval_raw <- smry$s.table[1, "p-value"]
    
    edf_label <- format(edf_val, nsmall = 1, digits = 1)
    pval_label <- case_when(
      pval_raw < 0.01 ~ "< 0.01",
      pval_raw > 0.99 ~ "> 0.99",
      TRUE ~ format(pval_raw, nsmall = 2, digits = 2))
    
    pdf(NULL)
    plot_data_raw <- plot(model, select = 1, seWithMean = TRUE, rug = FALSE)
    dev.off()
    
    if (length(plot_data_raw) == 0) return(NULL)
    
    plot_data <- plot_data_raw[[1]]
    smooth_df <- data.frame(
      x = plot_data$x,
      fit = plot_data$fit,
      se = plot_data$se)
    
    p1 <- ggplot(smooth_df, aes(x = x, y = fit)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "steelblue") +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = fit - 2 * se, 
                      ymax = fit + 2 * se),
                  alpha = 0.2, fill = "steelblue") +
      labs(
        title = title,
        y = "LogHR (smooth estimate)",
        x = NULL) +
      annotate(
        "text",
        x = -Inf, y = Inf,
        hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5,
        label = paste0("EDF: ", edf_label, "\np-value: ", pval_label),
        size = 4.2) +
      scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank())
    
    p2 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[var]], y = 1)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.3, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
      labs(x = vars_labels[[var]]) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  
    list(
      model = model,
      pval_raw = pval_raw,
      plot = p1 / p2 + plot_layout(heights = c(10, 1)))
  }
  
  results <- map(vars, fit_one)
  names(results) <- vars_labels[vars]
  
  return(results)
}

# Creation of cases specific datasets ----
bdd_cases_danish <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  filter(als == 1) |>                                                           # remove controls 
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>   
  mutate(across(all_of(proteomic),                                              # creation of proteomic standardized variables (cohort and cases specific)  
                ~as.numeric(scale(.x)),
                .names = "{.col}_sd")) 

surv_obj <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish$follow_up_death,                       # set the outcomes
                 event = bdd_cases_danish$status_death)

var_label(bdd_cases_danish[proteomic]) <- NULL

vars_labels <- 
  set_names(str_replace(proteomic, 
                        "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_", 
                        ""), 
            proteomic)


### Base ----
cox_gam_results_base <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels)

### Adjusted ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("sex", "diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  main_base  = plot_base_cox_gam_danish$signif, 
  main_adj  = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(bdd_cases_danish, surv_obj, vars_labels,  
   cox_gam_results_base, cox_gam_results_adjusted,  plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish)

```

## Sensi 1 + 3 : Removing NEFL outlier + filtering cases with follow-up \> 5 years

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=5}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-6
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_3 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-7
#| column: screen-inset-right
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_3, 
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_3, 
           ncol = 2)

```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-8
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_3  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=60, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-9
bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3 <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  filter (als == 1 & follow_up > 60) |>                                         # filtering cases with follow_up > 5 years
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) 

surv_obj_sensi_1_3 <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3$follow_up_death,   # set the outcomes
                           event = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3$status_death)

vars_labels <- set_names(str_replace(proteomic, 
                                     "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_", 
                                     ""), 
                         proteomic)


### Base  ----
cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3 <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels)


### Adjusted  ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3 <- 
    fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3, 
                         vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                         covariates = c("sex", "diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                         title = "Adjusted model")


plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  main = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif,
  sensi_1_3 = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3, surv_obj_sensi_1_3, vars_labels,  
   cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3, cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3, 
   plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid)
```

## Sensi 1 + 3 + 4 : Removing NEFL outlier + filtering cases with follow-up \> 5 years + filtering follow-up \< 50%

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=3}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-10
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_4 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-11
#| column: screen-inset-right
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_3_4,
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_3_4, 
           ncol = 2)

```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-12
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_4$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_4  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=100, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-13
bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4 <- bdd_danish |>
  filter(als == 1 & follow_up > 60) |>                                          # remove controls and remove cases with follow-up<5years 
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>   
  mutate(seuil = quantile(follow_up, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)) |>                     # sensi 4 : we remove 50% of the highest values of follow-up (after removing the follow-up<5years)
  filter(follow_up <= seuil) 

surv_obj_sensi_1_3_4 <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4$follow_up_death,           # set the outcomes
                             event = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4$status_death)


vars_labels <- set_names(str_replace(proteomic, 
                                     "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_", 
                                     ""), 
                         proteomic)


### Base  ----
cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_4 <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels)


### Adjusted  ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_4 <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("sex", "diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_4 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_4 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_4, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  main = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif,
  sensi_1_3 = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_4$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_4, surv_obj_sensi_1_3_4, vars_labels, 
   cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_4, cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_4, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_4, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_4, 
   plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid)
```

## Sensi 1 + 3 + 5 : Removing NEFL outlier + filtering cases with follow-up \> 5 years + filtering follow-up \> 50%

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=3}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-14
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_5 |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-15
#| column: screen-inset-right
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_3_5,
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_3_5, 
           ncol = 2)

```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-16
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_3_5$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_3_5  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=80, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-17
bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5 <- bdd_danish |>
  filter(als == 1 & follow_up > 60) |>                                          # remove controls and remove cases with follow-up<5years 
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>   
  mutate(seuil = quantile(follow_up, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)) |>                     # sensi 4 : we remove 50% of the highest values of follow-up (after removing the follow-up<5years)
  filter(follow_up > seuil) 

surv_obj_sensi_1_3_5 <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5$follow_up_death,           # set the outcomes
                             event = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5$status_death)


vars_labels <- set_names(str_replace(proteomic, 
                                     "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_", 
                                     ""), 
                         proteomic)


### Base  ----
cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_5 <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels)


### Adjusted  ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_5 <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("sex", "diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_5 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_5 <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_5, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))

plots_lists <- list(
  main = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif,
  sensi_1_3_5 = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_5$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_3_5, surv_obj_sensi_1_3_5, vars_labels, 
   cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_3_5, cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_3_5, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_5, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_3_5, 
   plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid)

```

## Sensi 1 + 7 females : Removing NEFL outlier + filtering female cases

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=3}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-18
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_female$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_7_female |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-19
#| column: screen-inset-right
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_female$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_7_female,
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_female$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_7_female, 
           ncol = 2)

```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-20
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_female$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_7_female  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=80, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-21
bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female <- 
  bdd_danish |>
  filter(als == 1) |>                                                           # remove controls
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>   
  filter(sex == "Female")                                                    # sensi 7 female : we purrr::keep only female cases
  

surv_obj_sensi_1_7_female <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female$follow_up_death,           # set the outcomes
                                  event = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female$status_death)


vars_labels <- set_names(str_replace(proteomic,
                                     "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
                                     ""),
                         proteomic)


### Base  ----
cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_female <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = "diagnosis_age")


### Adjusted  ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_female <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_female <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_female <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_female, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))


plots_lists <- list(
  main = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif,
  sensi_1_7_female = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_female$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_female, surv_obj_sensi_1_7_female, vars_labels, 
   cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_female, cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_female, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_female, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_female, 
   plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, vars_labels)
```

## Sensi 1 + 7 males : Removing NEFL outlier + male cases

### continuous standardized

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=3}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-22
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_male$proteomic_sd_ALS_table_sensi_1_7_male |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

```{r, fig.width=15, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-23
#| column: screen-inset-right
wrap_plots(results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_male$proteomic_sd_ALS_base_figure_sensi_1_7_male,
           results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_male$proteomic_sd_ALS_adjusted_figure_sensi_1_7_male, 
           ncol = 2)

```

### quartiles

```{r}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-24
#| column: screen-inset-right

results_proteomic_ALS_survival$sensi_1_7_male$proteomic_quart_ALS_table_sensi_1_7_male  |>
  set_table_properties(width = 1, layout = "autofit", align = "left")
```

### GAMs

```{r fig.height=80, fig.width=9}
#| label: danish-proteomic-ALS-survival-25
bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male <- bdd_danish |>
  filter(als == 1) |>                                                           # remove controls
  mutate(                                                                       # remove NEFL outlier
    proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL = ifelse(match == 159, NA, proteomic_neuro_explo_NEFL)) |>   
  filter(sex == "Male")                                                         # sensi 7 male : we purrr::keep only male cases


surv_obj_sensi_1_7_male <- Surv(time = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male$follow_up_death,           # set the outcomes
                                event = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male$status_death)

vars_labels <- set_names(str_replace(proteomic,
                                     "proteomic_immun_res_|proteomic_neuro_explo_|proteomic_metabolism_",
                                     ""),
                         proteomic)


### Base  ----
cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_male <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = "diagnosis_age")


### Adjusted  ----
cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_male <- 
  fit_and_plot_cox_gam(data = bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male, 
                       vars_labels = vars_labels, 
                       covariates = c("diagnosis_age", "smoking_2cat_i", "bmi"), 
                       title = "Adjusted model")

plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_male <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))
plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_male <- list(
  signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$pval_raw <= 0.05), "plot"),
  not_signif = map(purrr::keep(cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_male, ~ .x$pval_raw > 0.05), "plot"))

plots_lists <- list(
  main = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish$signif,
  sensi_1_7_male = plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_male$signif)

all_proteins <- sort(unique(unlist(lapply(plots_lists, names))))

plot_grid <- lapply(all_proteins, function(prot) {
  lapply(plots_lists, function(lst) {
    if (prot %in% names(lst)) {
      lst[[prot]]
    } else {
      wrap_elements(grid::nullGrob())
    }
  })
})

wrap_plots(
  unlist(plot_grid, recursive = FALSE),
  ncol = 2) 

rm(plot_base_cox_gam_danish, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish, 
   bdd_cases_danish_sensi_1_7_male, surv_obj_sensi_1_7_male, vars_labels, 
   cox_gam_results_base_sensi_1_7_male, cox_gam_results_adjusted_sensi_1_7_male, 
   plot_base_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_male, plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish_sensi_1_7_male, 
   plots_lists, all_proteins, plot_grid, 
   plot_adjusted_cox_gam_danish)

```


# POPs and proteomic 
```{r, fig.height=6}
#| label: danish-POPs-proteomic-1
#| column: screen-inset-right
results_POPs_proteomic$main$POPs_sd_proteomic_table
results_POPs_proteomic$main$POPs_sd_proteomic_figure
```


```{r}
#| label: danish-POPs-proteomic-2
results_POPs_proteomic$main$POPs_sd_NfL_table
```
